name: customer-profiles
title: Customers API
description: This API can be used to retrieve, create, update and delete customers as well as customer information.

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to retrieve a list of customers for a specific store. Deleted customers are excluded by default.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: An identifier for a store

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page of results to return for pagination. Only supported up to the first 10 000 records of a result set. Use pageToken rather than page where possible. PageToken and page can not be used in combination, doing so will result in a 422 response.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return. If used with page will return results with an offset of page * pageSize

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: Use the `pageToken` returned from a previous search in order to obtain the next set of results. Use pageToken rather than page where possible. PageToken and page can not be used in combination, doing so will result in a 422 response.

parameters.name: totalRequired
parameters.description: Indicates whether the response should include the total number of items and pages through response properties totalItems and totalPages respectively.

parameters.name: queryString
parameters.description: Search term - this string will be matched as a starts with for all text fields

parameters.name: nonEmptyFields
parameters.description: Filter customers by presence of at least one of these fields

parameters.name: emptyFields
parameters.description: Filter customers by absence of at least one of these fields

parameters.name: allNonEmptyFields
parameters.description: Filter customers by presence of all of these fields

parameters.name: allEmptyFields
parameters.description: Filter customers by absence of all of these fields

parameters.name: externalIdentifiers
parameters.description: Filter customers by external identifier; provide as comma separated values in the form `type:value` (eg: `poynt:123,asin:B0B9F7WNQH`)

items.description: External identifier type and value separated by a colon, eg `poynt:123`

parameters.name: updatedAtStart
parameters.description: Filter customers whose updatedAt is >= the specified date

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

parameters.name: updatedAtEnd
parameters.description: Filter customers whose updatedAt is <= the specified date

schema.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

totalItems.description: The total number of items in the full result set. Only returned if the `totalRequired` param is true

totalPages.description: The total number of pages of results in the full result set. Only returned if the `totalRequired` param is true

allOf.description: Customer identifier that is unique within current Store

nickname.description: Name by which customer should be addressed

addresses.description: Array of customer addresses. Note that currently only one address is supported

label.description: A descriptive name for the address

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

label.description: A user provided name for the email address

default.description: Indicates whether the email is the primary email.

instantMessages.description: Array of instant messaging accounts associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the instant messaging account

label.description: A user provided name for the external service

socialProfiles.description: Array of social profiles associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the social profile

label.description: A user provided name for the external service
A user provided name for the phone number

default.description: Indicates whether the phone is the primary phone.

urls.description: Array of urls associated with the customer

url.description: A url associated with the customer

label.description: A user provided name for the url

updatedAt.description: A date and time, in [Internet date and time format](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc3339#section-5.6). Note: The regular expression provides static schematic guidance but does not reject all invalid dates.

listKeys.description: List memberships that should be changed for the customer

joined.description: Whether the customer has been joined with one or more other customers to create a single customer

trackingDisabled.description: Whether the customer has opted out of tracking (eg. email opens and link clicks)

orderCount.description: Number of orders the customer has made

profileImageUrl.description: Url of the profile images for the customer

paymentCount.description: Number of payments the customer has completed

401.description: Authentication info not sent or is invalid

code.description: Short identifier for the error, suitable for indicating the specific error within client code

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

path.description:
1) JSONPath referring to the field within the data containing an error
or
2) JSONPath referring to an object containing an error

pathRelated.description: JSONPath referring to the field on the object referenced by `path` containing an error

code.description: Short identifier for the error, suitable for indicating the specific error within client code

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

fields.description: List of the specific fields, and the errors found with their contents

stack.description:
Stack trace indicating where the error occurred.
NOTE: This attribute MAY be included for Development and Test environments. However, it MUST NOT be exposed from OTE nor Production systems

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to create or de-duplicate - using channel data - and merge into existing customer. If the provided channels differ from existing customer data, a new customer will be created. This endpoint will not remove any existing data and is additive in cases where merges occur.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: An identifier for a store

allOf.description: Customer identifier that is unique within current Store

nickname.description: Name by which customer should be addressed

addresses.description: Array of customer addresses. Note that currently only one address is supported

label.description: A descriptive name for the address

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

label.description: A user provided name for the email address

default.description: Indicates whether the email is the primary email.

instantMessages.description: Array of instant messaging accounts associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the instant messaging account

label.description: A user provided name for the external service

socialProfiles.description: Array of social profiles associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the social profile

label.description: A user provided name for the external service
A user provided name for the phone number

default.description: Indicates whether the phone is the primary phone.

urls.description: Array of urls associated with the customer

url.description: A url associated with the customer

label.description: A user provided name for the url

customer.description: Whether or not the customer has completed transactions with the user

suppressed.description: Whether the customer is suppressed, due to opting out from email marketing, or their email address being unreachable

trackingDisabled.description: Whether the customer has opted out of tracking (eg. email opens and link clicks)

externalId.description: The external id for the customer in another service

source.description: The name of service from which the external id originates

listKeys.description: List memberships that should be changed for the customer

202.description: A CUSTOMER_CREATE action was successfully created

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to update the information of a specific customer using the store and customer ID.

parameters.name: customerId
parameters.description: The id of the specific customer

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: An identifier for a store

allOf.description: Customer identifier that is unique within current Store

nickname.description: Name by which customer should be addressed

addresses.description: Array of customer addresses. Note that currently only one address is supported

label.description: A descriptive name for the address

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

label.description: A user provided name for the email address

default.description: Indicates whether the email is the primary email.

instantMessages.description: Array of instant messaging accounts associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the instant messaging account

label.description: A user provided name for the external service

socialProfiles.description: Array of social profiles associated with the customer

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to combine two or more customers together to create a single customer. customerIds.description: IDs of the customer records affected - created or updated - by the request

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to create an actvity for a specific customer using the store ID. Customer activities are the series of actions a customer can perform such as orders, bookings, and payments, which can be tracked for further analysis. Customer data will only be added when the customer does not yet exist.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: An identifier for a store

type.description: The type of activity to associate with the customer

customer.description: Customer data regarding the customer who performed the activity. Customer data will only be added when the customer does not yet exist.

allOf.description: Customer identifier that is unique within current Store

nickname.description: Name by which customer should be addressed

addresses.description: Array of customer addresses. Note that currently only one address is supported

label.description: A descriptive name for the address

email.description: A valid, internationalized email address. Note: Up to 64 characters are allowed before and 255 characters are allowed after the @ sign. However, the generally accepted maximum length for an email address is 254 characters. The pattern verifies that an unquoted @ sign exists.

label.description: A user provided name for the email address

default.description: Indicates whether the email is the primary email.

instantMessages.description: Array of instant messaging accounts associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the instant messaging account

label.description: A user provided name for the external service

socialProfiles.description: Array of social profiles associated with the customer

accountKey.description: An accountKey for the social profile

label.description: A user provided name for the external service
A user provided name for the phone number

default.description: Indicates whether the phone is the primary phone.

urls.description: Array of urls associated with the customer

url.description: A url associated with the customer

label.description: A user provided name for the url

externalId.description: The external id for the customer in another service

source.description: The name of service from which the external id originates

listKeys.description: List memberships that should be changed for the customer

204.description: An activity for the given contact has been created

401.description: Authentication info not sent or is invalid

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

path.description:
1) JSONPath referring to the field within the data containing an error
or
2) JSONPath referring to an object containing an error

pathRelated.description: JSONPath referring to the field on the object referenced by `path` containing an error

code.description: Short identifier for the error, suitable for indicating the specific error within client code

message.description: Human-readable, English description of the error

fields.description: List of the specific fields, and the errors found with their contents

stack.description:
Stack trace indicating where the error occurred.
NOTE: This attribute MAY be included for Development and Test environments. However, it MUST NOT be exposed from OTE nor Production systems
